pages.title: Home
pages.purpose: High-level overview of the invalid-bitting-generator system, its purpose as a key bitting analysis tool for locksmithing research, and main capabilities

pages.title: Getting Started
pages.purpose: Practical guide for users to begin using the system, covering installation, CLI commands, and basic usage patterns

pages.title: CLI Commands
pages.purpose: Comprehensive reference for all command-line interface commands (check, repair, list-terminal, enumerate, presets) with arguments and examples

pages.title: Quick Start Examples
pages.purpose: Step-by-step walkthrough of common usage scenarios with real bitting sequences and expected outputs

pages.title: Core Concepts
pages.purpose: Foundational terminology and theoretical principles underlying the system, including key bitting, terminal invalidity, and constraint theory

pages.title: Key Bitting Terminology
pages.purpose: Define essential terms: bitting sequences, stations, depths, MACS, deepening, and lock-specific vocabulary

pages.title: Terminal Invalidity Theory
pages.purpose: Explain the concept of terminal invalidity, why it occurs, and its relationship to upward-closure in order theory

pages.title: Physical basis
pages.purpose: Repairs can only deepen cuts (never shallower), and how this constraint creates the terminal invalidity problem

pages.title: Constraint Classification
pages.purpose: Distinguish between monotone constraints (always repairable) and non-monotone constraints (can cause terminal invalidity)

pages.title: System Architecture
pages.purpose: Technical overview of the system's design, components, data flow, and algorithm selection mechanism

pages.title: Component Overview
pages.purpose: Describe the layered architecture: CLI layer, domain model (KeySystem), business logic (validation/repair), and algorithms

pages.title: Data Flow Pipeline
pages.purpose: Trace how bitting sequences flow through pre-validation, repair, and post-validation stages with decision points

pages.title: Algorithm Architecture
pages.purpose: Compare the dual-algorithm design: iterative constraint propagation vs Bellman-Ford constraint solver, and when to use each

pages.title: Lock Types and Presets
pages.purpose: Comprehensive catalog of 50+ lock system presets organized by mechanism type, with research sources for each

pages.title: Pin Tumbler Locks
pages.purpose: Document pin tumbler presets including generic (US, Euro) and manufacturer-specific (Kwikset, Schlage, Yale, BEST) configurations

pages.title: Warded Locks
pages.purpose: Document warded lock presets, distinguishing realistic variants (multiward, lever, padlock) from educational simplified variants

pages.title: Disc Tumbler Locks
pages.purpose: Document Abloy disc tumbler presets (Classic, Protec, Protec2, Disklock, Exec, Profile) with focus on consecutive repeat constraints

pages.title: Tubular Locks
pages.purpose: Document tubular pin tumbler presets, including standard (4-10 pin) and vintage (Chicago, Ace) configurations

pages.title: Wafer Tumbler Locks
pages.purpose: Document wafer tumbler presets including generic, automotive (GM, Ford, Chrysler), and furniture lock variants

pages.title: Constraint System
pages.purpose: Detailed technical specification of the KeySystem constraint model and all constraint types

pages.title: KeySystem Model
pages.purpose: Document the KeySystem dataclass structure with all fields: d_min, d_max, macs, length, forbidden_positions, station_max_ceiling, stop_type, etc.

pages.title: Monotone Constraints
pages.purpose: Explain MACS and global depth bounds, why they're monotone under deepening, and how they're always repairable

pages.title: Non-Monotone Constraints
pages.purpose: Explain forbidden stations, station-specific ceilings, first station restrictions, and consecutive repeat limits—why they cause terminal invalidity

pages.title: Stop Type System
pages.purpose: Explain shoulder-stop vs tip-stop measurement datums and how they affect first station constraints and key cutting conventions

pages.title: Algorithms
pages.purpose: Deep dive into validation and repair algorithms, their implementation, complexity, and theoretical foundations

pages.title: Validation Pipeline
pages.purpose: Document the three-stage validation process: pre-validation, repair attempt, post-validation with fast-fail optimization

pages.title: Iterative Constraint Propagation
pages.purpose: Explain the default algorithm: bidirectional forward/backward passes, convergence detection, O(n²) complexity

pages.title: Bellman-Ford Constraint Solver
pages.purpose: Explain the difference constraints formulation, Bellman-Ford propagation, negative cycle detection, and when to use --use-bellman-ford

pages.title: Algorithm Comparison
pages.purpose: Compare iterative vs Bellman-Ford approaches: complexity, theoretical foundations, practical tradeoffs, and equivalence proof

pages.title: Research Integration
pages.purpose: Document how the system grounds its constraint models in real manufacturer specifications rather than theoretical assumptions
